yarma-soup-with-yogurt-lezzet.name: Yarma Soup With Yogurt
instructions: 1. Mix the moon well with salt and flour in a large pot.
2. Wash the peninsula for 10 minutes in a separate saucepan and add it to the island.
3. Cook it continuously stirring with wooden spoon until the jealous fire is lost in order to prevent the cut of the moon.
4. Goodbyes; get from the stove to soften.
5. Melt the butter in a separate pan and add crushed garlic and paste.
6.  Hafif kavurun.
7. Add the prepared season to the soup and boil for 5 minutes.
8. Serve and serve with pepper.
9.  Afiyet olsun.
10. If you wish, you can also try the recipe of the yarma soup presented in a simple way.

yucatecan-salbutes-traditional-recipe-co.name: Yucatecan Salbutes, Traditional Recipe

ingredients.item: 1 / 2 kilo omelette corn mass (as prescribed by comedera.com)

instructions: Para comenzar, cortaremos las naranjas en mitades y las exprimiremos.
Luego, mezclaremos el jugo con el achiote y un punto de sal y pimienta.
When we get a uniform mix, we'll put up with the chicken. The longer we leave him at the marines, the more flavour will be absorbed. Ideally, leave them at least an hour.
Mientras tanto, podemos aprovechar para desinfectar las hojas de lechuga. Yo las pongo a remojar con agua y vinagre. Luego las pasaremos por agua fresca, las escurriremos y las cortaremos en juliana.
In the case of the tomato, we'll wash them well and cut them into tires. We will also peel half an onion and cut it into pieces.
To prepare carrots, you can use the time that chicken takes to sail and skin and cook them in hot water with salt. I'd rather cut them into wheels before we cook them so they're ready more quickly.
En el caso del aguacate, simplemente hay que cortar la pulpa en rebanadas delgadas.

If you decide to use cucumbers, just peel and cut them into very thin cuts. I cut every slice in half, to have less space. In fact, this ingredient isn't so characteristic of the traditional recipe for salbuts, but I really love the freshness that it brings to the plate.
Reforms the marinated breast to be roasted in a paddle, plate or frying pan. When I'm ready, we're gonna get her broken or broken into filaments with the help of a couple of forks.
Then we will take the dough of corn we have prepared with the omelette recipe from Comedera.com and we will do the traditional round form of each omelette, helping with a roller or press.
Then we'll warm up some butter in a big frying pan and then we'll fry our tortillas up till they get golden and start pounding.
At the time of withdrawing them from frying pan, I would rather place them in a plate covered with an absorbing paper sheet to remove excess fat.
We will prepare each salbut with a little chicken on each omelette with onion, tomato, lettuce, avocado and carrot and optionally with half a slice of cucumber.

yum-phonlamai.name: Yum Phonlamai

ingredients.prep: preferably Pink Lady, cored, cut into 1" pieces

instructions: If using, toast dried shrimp in a dry small skillet, tossing occasionally, over medium heat until slightly lighter in appearance and a little crispy, about 4 minutes. Transfer to a cutting board; reserve skillet. Coarsely chop and lightly smash to tenderize; set aside.
Add coconut to reserved skillet and toast over medium heat until light golden and fragrant, about 2 minutes. Transfer to a small bowl; set aside.

Smash garlic to a coarse paste with a mortar and pestle. Add chiles and lightly smash (the finer you pound the chiles, the spicier the dressing will be). Mix in lime juice, sugar, fish sauce, and 2 Tbsp. water. (Or, finely chop garlic and salt, then chiles, on a cutting board and smash with the side of a chef’s knife. Mix in a bowl with remaining ingredients.)
Toss apples, pears, shallot, grapes, cilantro, mint, and reserved dried shrimp (if using) in a large bowl to combine. Add dressing and toss to coat. Top with reserved toasted coconut.
